attempt_summary: Normalized actual outputs from artifact files.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Include preserved, readable formulas or an exported calculation sheet (CSV with formula references or an inline calculation section) so all intermediate computations (baseline aggregation, control_gap_pct, per-record computations, cost totals) can be independently recomputed. Correct the baseline aggregation discrepancy and explicitly show the workbook cell ranges used to compute baseline, mitigated, and sensitivity metrics. Provide explicit budget line items or a short cost table in controls_setpoint.md referencing workbook cells.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Both required artifacts are present. The markdown documents an explicit decision table, recommendations, and record-level flags. However the workbook could not be parsed for formula verification (openpyxl unavailable) and the markdown contains numeric inconsistencies (baseline control_gap_pct average reported as 71.5% while record-level values imply ~101.9%). Cost accounting and intermediate formulae are not independently verifiable from previews. Therefore reproducibility and numeric traceability are not confirmed.

attempt_summary: Normalized actual outputs from artifact files.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Open and supply a parseable workbook or include an exported worksheet showing cell formulas and ranges (or provide an explicit sheet/cell reference map) so intermediate calculations (per-record metric computations, cost aggregation, sensitivity shifts) are directly inspectable. Ensure explicit line-item cost calculations are present in the workbook and reference the embedded-packet R1..R4 or limit_* inputs in cell comments or adjacent citation columns.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Required artifacts are present. controls_setpoint.md preview contains selected setpoints, a baseline/mitigated/sensitivity decision table, a needs_review label for R4, validation checks, and an expert note explaining the zero incremental implementation cost temporary stand-in. ActualOutputs metadata reports a mitigated improvement of ~54.97% and implementation_cost_usd = 0 with within_budget=true, satisfying the budget (<= 70000 USD) and improvement (>= 10%) success criteria. However, the workbook (greenhouse_climate_tradeoff.xlsx) could not be inspected for preserved formulas/executable logic (openpyxl unavailable in the preview environment), so reproducibility of intermediate calculations could not be independently verified.
